prd.oneLiner: Connect incident intake, service impact, response roles, timelines, status communications, and post-incident review.
prd.goal: Reduce initial detection time for major incidents and achieve 90% or more post-incident action completion.
prd.whyNow: Incident reports, response discussion, status communications, and follow-up work are scattered across channels, slowing impact control and learning.
prd.problem: Incident reports, response discussion, status communications, and follow-up work are scattered across channels, slowing impact control and learning.
prd.solution: Connect incident intake, service impact, response roles, timelines, status communications, and post-incident review.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Service user reporting or affected by an incident
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Incident reports, response discussion, status communications, and follow-up work are scattered across channels, slowing impact control and learning.

targets.name: IT operations, SRE, and incident manager
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Incident reports, response discussion, status communications, and follow-up work are scattered across channels, slowing impact control and learning.

scenarios.text: Service user reporting or affected by an incident completes incident report and impact-scope submission and checks progress.
IT operations, SRE, and incident manager reviews mtta, mttr, recurring incident, and follow-up-action reporting.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Reduce initial detection time for major incidents and achieve 90% or more post-incident action completion.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of completion history against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of registered requests connected to operations reporting

inScope: Incident, service, timeline, owner, and status lookup
Severity triage, responder paging, status communication, and post-incident review handling
MTTA, MTTR, recurring incident, and follow-up-action reporting
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
